content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 8, 1988

John Mahan
1212 Los Padres Blvd.
Santa Clara, CA 95050

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-303

Dear Mr. Mahan:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974 (the
"Act").1/

QUESTIONS

1. May an existing controlled committee, originally
established for a mayoral campaign, be used for the controlling
candidate's campaign for school board?

2. Must the name of the treasurer be identified at this
time?

3. Are there any restrictions concerning who is chosen as
treasurer?

CONCLUSIONS

1. The existing committee may be used for the proposed
school board race, as long as the statement of organization
(Form 410) is amended to indicate the new purpose of the
committee. The committee would retain its I.D. number.

2. The name of the committee's treasurer must be on file
with the Secretary of State's office. If there is a vacancy in
the treasurer's office, the committee may not accept
contributions, nor make expenditures until the position is
filled.

3. There are no restrictions in the Act concerning who may
be a treasurer of a committee. However, the treasurer has
specified duties under the Act, must sign all reporting
statements and verify that information contained in the
statements is true and complete. A candidate may be his or her
own treasurer.

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Mahan, John
Page Two

FACTS

You currently have a controlled committee which you used
for your run for mayor. You are now a candidate for the Santa
Clara school board. You intend to change the name of your
committee from "Mahan for Mayor Committee" to "Friends of John
Mahan."

ANALYSIS

Section 84103 requires that an amendment be filed to the
statement of organization whenever there is a change in any of
the information contained in a statement of organization. This
amendment must be filed within 10 days after the change occurs.

Section 84100 states that:

Every committee shall have a treasurer. No expenditure
shall be made by or on behalf of a committee without the
authorization of the treasurer or that of his or her
designated agents. No contributions or expenditure shall
be accepted or made by or on behalf of a committee at a
time when there is a vacancy in the office of treasurer.

Enclosed, please find Commission regulation 18427, which
sets out the duties of candidates and treasurers with respect
to campaign statements.

If you have any further questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

5/17/78

Dear Sir                                    JUL 29 6 32 AM '08

I at the present time hold I.D # 830574
"Matuan for Mayor Committee"

I am now a candidate for the
South Clara School Board my new
Committee will be Friends of John Mehan.
Do I need a new I.D. number
or do you change the name on the
present one?

Do you need the name of a Treasurer
at this time?

Is there any restrictions ade I
collect?

                                          Sincerely
                                          John Mehan
                                          Mehan
                                          1212 Los Padres Blvd.
                                          Santa Clara
